mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where unemployment rises above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in more than 60% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are negative in more than 55% of acceleration periods or the signal shows no predictive power during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in imminent monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 60% of high-inflation months or the signal fails to predict direction during inflation spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate earnings growth and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in more than 55% of cases or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which translates into positive short-term S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over 2-year rolling windows or positive returns occur in less than 50% of high-GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction and weakening supply-side momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of contraction periods or the signal loses predictive accuracy during late-cycle phases.
